vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry baseline is largely unsupported by current visual evidence.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (String of Nickels): Systemic Loss. No visible foliage remains in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Mexican Mint): Systemic Loss. No viable plant matter detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Pothos): Systemic Loss. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent/non-viable.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Silver Guest): Systemic Loss. No evidence of the specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine remains, but the host plant is missing.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and inert across all containers.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit figurine is the only non-soil object present.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Initial state shows empty pots with a single sprout/stem fragment in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: The sprout/stem fragment has withered or been removed; total loss of visible plant matter.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered specimens have suffered total mortality or removal. The environment is currently a collection of empty vessels.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an otherwise empty biome suggests a failed or abandoned cultivation attempt.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided chronological sequence, it is evident that the botanical specimens have failed to thrive. The sequence begins with a single, struggling sprout in the black pot (P3/P4 area), which subsequently disappears. By the current timestamp, all pots are devoid of plant life. The biome is effectively dormant or dead.
